description: Round-trip vectors for POST /v2/permits:bulk-revoke (Pillar 8 — Temporal). Every SDK MUST: (a) serialize sdk_input into exactly wire_request on the wire — revoker_id MUST be omitted (not sent as null) when not provided; (b) when the server returns wire_response, produce sdk_output with idiomatic field names. revoked_count: 0 is not an error and MUST NOT be thrown.

vectors.name: bulk_revoke_with_active_permits
vectors.description: Happy path: three active permits exist for the run_id; all are revoked. SDK MUST echo workflow_id and run_id from the response.

sdk_input.reason: Workflow completed successfully — permits no longer needed

wire_request.reason: Workflow completed successfully — permits no longer needed

vectors.name: bulk_revoke_zero_permits
vectors.description: revoked_count: 0 is returned when no active permits exist for the run_id (already expired or consumed). SDK MUST NOT throw — 0 is the expected outcome for the race where permits expired before the signal fires.

vectors.name: bulk_revoke_no_revoker_id
vectors.description: revoker_id is optional. When the caller does not supply it, SDK MUST NOT include the key in the wire request (not null, not empty string — absent entirely). The server defaults revoker_id to workflow_id in the audit log.
